rationale: Both correctly land on the ground-truth resolution (duplicate of #4808), but X never mentions issue #4809, which the actual maintainer comment explicitly points to as the source of 'additional details' — a notable grounding gap. X also asserts flatly that #4808 is merged and the fix is on main, a confident claim with no clear support that risks being wrong; Y instead flags that #4808 shows closed/unmerged and asks for confirmation, which is the more defensible stance given the terse, non-committal ground-truth comment. Y is also more complete, surfacing the concrete regression-test coverage gap (_STALE_API_FILES missing the diffusion path) as a genuine actionable follow-up, whereas X's answer, while crisp and directly actionable ('git pull, retest'), is narrower and would mislead the reporter if the merge-status assumption is wrong.
